title: viftyctl --json command error report
description: Machine-readable command or viftyctl run wrapper failure emitted by viftyctl when --json is present. safeToProceed is always false for this report.

schemaID.description: Stable schema identity for this command-error payload. Agents can compare this with capabilities.schemaIDs.commandError before trusting command-error fields.

recommendedRecoveryAction.description: Machine-readable next safe step. Agents should use this field instead of parsing message text.

recoverySteps.description: Ordered human-safe recovery guidance for the recommendedRecoveryAction. Agents may show these steps directly, but must not treat them as cooling authorization.

coolingLeasePrepared.description: True only when viftyctl run successfully prepared a matching cooling lease before reporting this error.

autoRestoreAttempted.description: True when viftyctl run attempted to restore Auto after preparing a lease and then failing before the child workload could run.

autoRestoreSucceeded.description: null when no Auto restore was attempted; otherwise reports whether the run-wrapper cleanup restore succeeded.

childProcessFailurePhase.description: Present when the child supervisor can identify whether launch, leader wait, or descendant cleanup failed.

childExitCode.description: The leader's shell-style exit code when it was known before a later lifecycle failure.

descendantCleanupCompleted.description: False when the process group could not be confirmed gone before the Auto restore attempt.

backgroundProcessesMayRemain.description: True when bounded TERM/KILL escalation could not prove the child process group exited.

retryAfterSeconds.description: Present for PREPARE_RATE_LIMITED errors when Vifty can tell an agent how long to wait before retrying.

generatedAt.description: Swift JSONEncoder date value emitted by the CLI.
